title: Dense Roasted Greek Vegetable Casserole
description: A light and flavorful Greek dish featuring roasted vegetables and piccalilli, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This ovo-vegetarian friendly casserole is dense and satisfying.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the sliced eggplant and zucchini with salt to draw out excess moisture.
Let the vegetables sit for 10 minutes, then rinse and pat dry with paper towels.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs and a pinch of salt and pepper.
In a large skillet, roast the sliced red bell pepper over medium heat until tender, about 10 minutes.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of roasted bell pepper.
Add a layer of eggplant and zucchini slices, followed by a sprinkle of feta cheese, olives, and parsley.
Pour some of the egg mixture over the vegetables, then repeat the layering process until all ingredients are used, ending with a layer of egg mixture on top.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 40 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the top is golden brown.
Let the casserole rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving with a side of piccalilli.
